derivation.scope: Decision and gap log for turning source text into normalized ingestion data.

askUserWhenMissing: targeting mode or valid target type
aura source ownership or cancel behavior
summon ownership, limit, or lifecycle behavior

layerDecisions.decision: Keep source-normalized ingestion separate from mechanics-ready JSON and UI-ready JSON.
layerDecisions.reason: The prior combined definition file mixed source normalization, runtime mechanics, and UI flow into one heavy schema.

layerDecisions.decision: Use source-normalized ingestion for rule facts only, not TypeScript classes.
layerDecisions.reason: Action and Effect classes are implementation details; source text should normalize to game concepts.

layerDecisions.decision: Derive mechanics-ready and UI-ready JSON from normalized ingestion plus explicit user decisions.
layerDecisions.reason: This keeps future power ingestion auditable and prevents hidden assumptions from entering runtime code.

currentInterpretationDecisions.decision: Current mechanics still use spell id default for Boost Physique.
currentInterpretationDecisions.reason: The runtime already depends on this id. Source-normalized data records the display name separately.

currentInterpretationDecisions.decision: Release Control is contextual, not a normal cast-form spell.
currentInterpretationDecisions.reason: It is only meaningful when the caster already controls a target.

currentInterpretationDecisions.decision: Dismiss Summon is contextual for Necromancy and Shadow Control.
currentInterpretationDecisions.reason: It is summon-management behavior, not a normal spell cast. The encounter UI exposes it in the caster action menu when the caster controls a matching summon.

currentInterpretationDecisions.decision: Brute Defiance is passive-only, not a cast-form action.
currentInterpretationDecisions.reason: Its current behavior is triggered from HP and daily-use state, then resolved through delayed stand-up.

currentInterpretationDecisions.decision: Current UI exposes Non-Living Warriors as the parent Necromancy spell, then exposes summon subtypes as the Summon choice.
currentInterpretationDecisions.reason: This keeps the active cast flow aligned with Power > Spell > Summon while preserving subtype-specific summon templates.

currentInterpretationDecisions.decision: Artifact Appraisal now uses the inventory Identify shortcut on top of the shared world-casting and item-knowledge path.
currentInterpretationDecisions.reason: This keeps appraisal aligned with canonical item-card revision refresh, linked history rows, and existing DM share flows without adding a separate generic cast-form UI.

currentInterpretationDecisions.decision: Record the implemented temporary-inspiration behavior, but defer the source's uninspired-status interaction.
currentInterpretationDecisions.reason: The current sheet/runtime has temporary inspiration but no explicit uninspired status mechanic to clear.

knownGapsForFollowUp.gap: Resolved: active cast UI now shows the Spell step even when only one spell variant is available.
knownGapsForFollowUp.desiredResolution: Power > Spell is the stable active-cast baseline, followed only by spell-specific fields.

knownGapsForFollowUp.gap: Resolved: aura casts explicitly select beneficiaries where needed and linked effects remain tied to the caster-owned source aura.
knownGapsForFollowUp.desiredResolution: Removing the source aura clears source-linked beneficiary effects.

knownGapsForFollowUp.gap: Resolved: Dismiss Summon is exposed as a contextual caster action for owned Necromancy and Shadow Control summons.
knownGapsForFollowUp.desiredResolution: Dismiss Summon remains contextual summon-management UI rather than a normal cast-form spell.

knownGapsForFollowUp.gap: Resolved: Necromancy summon UI now uses Power > Spell: Non-Living Warriors > Summon subtype.
knownGapsForFollowUp.desiredResolution: Keep summon subtype choice in the Summon field under the parent spell.

futureIngestionChecklist: Normalize each source text spell into active, passive, or contextual.
Record availability and level progression before deriving mechanics JSON.
Record mechanics-critical costs, targeting, range, duration, and scaling.
Record required player inputs before deriving UI JSON.
Record source ambiguities in this decision log instead of silently choosing defaults.
Ask the user only for blocking gaps.
